audit_items.proof_trace: `Health()` locks the `_rpc_server` weak pointer into `server` at line 48 and guards `set_health()` with `if (server && server->IsListening())`, but unconditionally calls `server->GetStartTime()` at line 55 after the if-else block — outside any null guard. If the `RpcServerImpl` shared owner has been released (server shutdown), `server` is null and this dereference crashes the process. All sibling methods (`ServerOptions`, `ServerStatus`, `UpdateOptions`) correctly check `if (!server) return;` before using the pointer, confirming this is an oversight rather than intentional design.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A remote client sends a Health RPC call during server shutdown, when the `RpcServerImpl` ref count has dropped to zero making `_rpc_server.lock()` return null; the unconditional `server->GetStartTime()` at line 55 dereferences the null pointer, crashing the server process (denial of service).
